metaTitle: Checklist for Electrical Safety Audit in Mexico | Carelabs
metaDescription: An electrical safety audit checklist in Mexico is an essential way to determine whether your workers and work environment conditions adhere to electrical component safety rules.
h2: Benefits of Availing Service from Carelabs:
Integrating Safety and Compliance to Future-Proof Your Electrical System
paragraphs: The invisible nature of electrical power tends to give a false sense of security to people. There are many working people exposed to these hazard risks that they do not even realize the associated risks.
An Electrical Safety Audit- ESA is a systematic procedure to evaluate and assess the potential electrical hazard risks and to recommend suggestions for control and improvement.

It is important to have a full electrical safety audit performed by a professional electrician every once in a while. Employers are responsible for the safety of your employees and facility’s electrical installations.
Carelabs provides several services to ensure the safety, reliability and efficiency of electrical infrastructure. We offer comprehensive electrical safety audit service for all types of facilities.

An electrical safety checklist is a important checklist tool to determine if your working people and workplace conditions adhere to electrical safety compliance safety code, rules & regulations. This checklist provides an overall workplace safety assessment in managing electrical hazard risks.
Some of the check items on the electrical safety audit include:
Extension cords suitability and safe condition:

bulletLists: Services Arc Flash Study Relay Coordination Study Load Flow Analysis Short Circuit Analysis
Services Arc Flash Study Relay Coordination Study Load Flow Analysis Short Circuit Analysis
Identification and elimination of safety hazards
Prevent loss due to electrical hazards
Compliance with statutory safety standards, codes, and regulations
Ensuring longevity of your expensive equipment
Developing a dynamic record of safety performance
Creation of positive cycle of safety improvement
Assessing the insulation integrity of cables & wires by conducting insulation resistance tests on a sample basis
Review of the earthing system (installation & maintenance aspects), including sample earth resistance tests
Identifying the overloading areas by conducting load current measurements and verify it against current carrying capacity of cable calculation
Hotspot detection using hot spot detection equipment/ thermal imaging
Selection, installation, and maintenance of residual current circuit breaker (RCCB)
To check the wiring methods and their suitability
To check if all the circuit conductors are bundled together
To check the condition of the insulations and insulation standards
Any equipment or components has been left plugged in must be inspected to ensure that they are properly grounded.
To check the type of electrodes used for grounding.
To check the size of grounding electrodes and their maintenance condition.
To check the methods used for connections and grounding for the installations.
Test procedure and acceptable earth resistance values
History of modification of earthing system
Testing of earth continuity, bonding, and earthing
Storage of flammable materials near the equipment
Selection and usage of appropriate fire extinguishers
Overrated fuses and wrongly set protection relays
Check for the usage of extension cords of appropriate size and type for their intended purpose.
Inspect all the switches and outlets
Check for presence of faceplate in all power switches and outlets
Check for no loose cables are accessible without removing the faceplate.
Signs to notify the employers about PPE
The following checklist helps in the creation of a safe and reliable electrical system.
Applicability of international and local standards/ regulations
Compliance to inspection report and inspection before any new installation
Competency of advisors and objective of training
Knowledge of relevant standards during the program
Transformer and periodic calibration of meters
Are the protection relays set and coordinated?
Are the relays set by calculated parameters?
Are the relays calibrated and tested periodically?
Are HRC fuses, standard fuse wires, MCBs, MCCBs, RCCBs, etc. available
Develop a dynamic record and positive cycle of improvement
